CHK Oil ( (HK:0632) ) just unveiled an announcement.

CHK Oil Limited reported a sharp decline in revenue to HK$58.8 million for 2025 from HK$153.1 million a year earlier, but swung from a loss to a profit as it booked substantial reversals of previous impairments on property, plant, equipment and intangible assets. The group posted a net profit of HK$16.2 million versus a HK$21.5 million loss in 2024, with improved other income and lower administrative expenses helping offset weaker sales, resulting in positive earnings per share and signalling a partial recovery in its balance sheet and financial position despite ongoing top-line pressure.

Yunnan Jinxun Resources Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3636) ) just unveiled an update.

Yunnan Jinxun Resources Co., Ltd. reported strong growth for the year ended 31 December 2025, with revenue rising 28.3% to RMB2.27 billion and gross profit climbing 41.1% to RMB518.9 million. The company’s gross profit margin improved to 22.8%, and basic earnings per share increased to RMB2.98, underscoring improved operational efficiency and profitability.

Net profit surged 62.4% year on year to RMB328.8 million, lifting the net margin to 14.5% and highlighting the company’s strengthened earnings profile. data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Chiho Environmental Group Limited ( (HK:0976) ).

Chiho Environmental Group Limited, a major metal recycling company with significant operations across Asia and Europe, says its core recycling and related businesses remain broadly stable and are operating as usual in all material respects. The group, which plays a notable role in the international scrap metal and resource recovery market, notes that neither the identified irregularities at certain subsidiaries nor the continued suspension of trading have so far caused adverse operational disruption, based on the board’s preliminary assessment.

The company provided a quarterly update on its efforts to meet resumption requirements set by the Hong Kong Stock Exchange following the trading halt linked to those irregularities. It has submitted draft reports on an independent forensic investigation and an internal control review to the exchange, a key step toward satisfying resumption guidance and demonstrating improved governance, with further disclosures on findings and remedial actions to follow.

More about Chiho Environmental Group Limited

Chiho Environmental Group Limited is one of the world’s largest publicly listed metal recycling corporations, with operations spanning Asia and Europe. The group focuses on recycling and processing scrap metals, positioning itself as a key player in the circular economy and resource recovery value chain, and reports that its core businesses continue to run normally despite recent governance issues.

The company’s operational footprint and metal recycling activities have, according to its directors, not been materially affected by previously disclosed irregularities at certain subsidiaries or by the prolonged suspension of trading in its shares. Management continues to monitor any potential impact from these issues and has committed to updating investors as necessary.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: BGMC International Cuts Debt via Share-for-Credit Deal, Diluting Major Holders
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BGMC International Ltd. ( (HK:1693) ) has issued an announcement.

BGMC International has completed a debt capitalisation exercise, issuing 29,588,744 new shares to holders of redeemable secured loan stocks and 7,489,496 shares to trade creditors. The shares were priced at HK$2.320 and HK$2.350 respectively, with the full subscription amounts used to offset outstanding RSLS and trade payables, meaning no new cash proceeds were raised.

Following the transaction, the company’s issued share capital increased from 43,200,000 to 80,278,240 shares, significantly diluting existing substantial shareholders’ combined stake from a majority position to a smaller proportion of the enlarged base. The move broadens the public float, makes key suppliers and contractors meaningful shareholders, and reduces balance-sheet debt, potentially easing financial pressure while reshaping control dynamics among major and public investors.

More about BGMC International Ltd.

BGMC International Ltd. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through its construction-related subsidiaries such as BGMC Holdings Berhad. The group works with a wide network of suppliers and contractors, including over 300 parties that now also hold shares in the company, reflecting its reliance on an extended project delivery chain.

WuXi XDC Cayman Inc. and BioDlink International Company Limited announced the close of voluntary conditional cash offers for WuXi XDC shares and share options, which were made on behalf of the offeror by Citigroup Global Markets Asia. The offeror secured valid acceptances for about 61.47% of WuXi XDC’s issued share capital and approximately 99.74% of outstanding share options, resulting in majority control and the cancellation of most options, reshaping the company’s ownership and capital structure.

Following completion and subject to registration of transfers, the offeror and its concert parties now control roughly 61.47% of WuXi XDC’s voting rights, consolidating influence over corporate decisions. The offers were neither revised nor extended past the 31 March 2026 deadline, leaving only 20,000 share options outstanding and signaling a completed change of control that may affect minority shareholders’ position and future governance dynamics.

data.title: Times China Advances Debt Restructuring as Audit Disclaimers Highlight Going-Concern Risks
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Times China Holdings ( (HK:1233) ) has shared an update.

Times China Holdings, a Hong Kong-listed Chinese property developer, continues to navigate financial stress after its auditors issued disclaimers of opinion on its 2024 and 2025 consolidated results due to material going-concern uncertainties. The company has set out and is implementing a series of action plans aimed at resolving these audit qualifications and stabilising its capital structure.

The Group reports progress on its offshore debt restructuring, with sizeable portions of zero-coupon mandatory convertible bonds converted into new shares, effectively reducing overall debt levels. It has also issued new senior notes to settle accrued interest in kind, conserving cash for operations, while soliciting creditor consent to amend covenants and waive potential defaults.

Alongside offshore measures, Times China is considering restructuring its onshore debts in the PRC and is in active talks with lenders over renewals, extensions, or further restructuring of borrowings. Management is also pursuing new financing, boosting property sales and collections, cutting administrative and capital costs, and exploring asset disposals to strengthen liquidity and support project delivery.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1233) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.09 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Times China Holdings stock, see the HK:1233 Stock Forecast page.

More about Times China Holdings

Times China Holdings is a Hong Kong-listed property developer incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating primarily through subsidiaries in mainland China. The Group focuses on developing and selling residential and mixed-use property projects, and relies heavily on ongoing access to offshore and onshore financing to support construction, debt servicing, and overall liquidity management.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Time Watch Investments Ltd. ( (HK:2033) ) has issued an announcement.

Time Watch Investments Limited has revised the annual cap for its continuing connected transactions under a master supply agreement with the ILG Group, reflecting stronger demand for watch movements and OEM-manufactured watches. The company expects existing limits to be insufficient for the higher order volume and is increasing the cap while keeping all other contractual terms unchanged.

Because ILG is deemed a connected person at the subsidiary level through its ownership links with Time Watch’s indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary, the transactions fall under Hong Kong Listing Rules on continuing connected transactions. The revised annual cap exceeds the 5% threshold, triggering reporting and announcement requirements, but the deal is exempt from independent shareholders’ approval as the board, including independent non-executive directors, has deemed the terms fair, reasonable and in the interests of shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2033) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.29 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Time Watch Investments Ltd. stock, see the HK:2033 Stock Forecast page.

More about Time Watch Investments Ltd.

Time Watch Investments Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the watch industry. The group supplies watch movements and finished watches, including products manufactured on an OEM basis, and serves both its own brands and external customers such as the ILG Group.

KWG Group Holdings ( (HK:1813) ) has shared an announcement.

KWG Group Holdings reported a sharp decline in revenue to RMB 6.76 billion for the year ended 31 December 2025, down from RMB 11.06 billion a year earlier, as China’s property downturn continued to pressure sales and margins. The group’s gross profit fell to RMB 1.01 billion, while fair value losses on investment properties, higher finance costs and losses from associates and joint ventures contributed to a net loss of RMB 3.46 billion, although this narrowed from RMB 8.15 billion in 2024.

Despite a sizeable increase in other income and gains to RMB 4.68 billion and reduced selling, marketing and administrative expenses, the company remained deeply loss-making, with basic and diluted loss per share at RMB 0.96. The results highlight ongoing stress on the developer’s balance sheet and profitability, underscoring persistent challenges for creditors and shareholders amid weak market demand and asset revaluations in the Chinese real estate sector.

Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) ( (HK:0444) ) has shared an update.

Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) reports that its core watch distribution business remains under pressure from soft luxury demand and unresolved talks with international brands over key commercial terms, while its PRC property investment operations continue on a steady but non-expansive basis. Management is prioritising risk control and business diversification to stabilise income streams in a challenging macroeconomic backdrop.

Sundy Service Group Co. Ltd ( (HK:9608) ) has issued an announcement.

Sundy Service Group reported 2025 revenue of RMB231.4 million, down 6.5% year on year, with its main property management segment contributing 81.2% of sales and all four business lines posting revenue declines. Despite softer top-line performance and a slight reduction in cash and cash equivalents, gross profit rose 18.1% and margin improved to 29.3%, while net profit more than doubled to RMB17.4 million on lower administrative expenses, with managed gross floor area remaining stable and no final dividend proposed.

The Group’s property management services revenue fell 4.3% to RMB187.8 million, while value-added services to non-property owners nearly halved and community value-added services and other businesses recorded mid-single-digit declines. The company’s stable managed and contracted GFA, combined with significantly better profitability and a continued dividend suspension, suggests a focus on cost control and operational efficiency amid a challenging revenue environment for the property management sector.

Winfair Investment Co., Ltd. ( (HK:0287) ) has shared an announcement.

Winfair Investment Company Limited has announced the resignation of independent non-executive director (INED) Ms. Chan Suit Fei, Esther, effective 31 March 2026, in order to comply with Hong Kong listing tenure requirements and to avoid potential conflicts of interest linked to a possible leasing arrangement involving her personal business and connected persons. Her departure also triggers changes in committee roles, as she steps down from the chairmanship and membership of the Nomination Committee and relinquishes her positions on the Audit, Remuneration and Corporate Governance Committees, with the board noting she has no disagreements or issues requiring shareholder attention.

On the same date, the company has appointed Mr. Yip Sing as an INED and member of the Audit, Remuneration, Nomination and Corporate Governance Committees, strengthening the board’s financial expertise with his more than 15 years of experience in accounting, auditing, internal control and financial consultancy. In related governance adjustments, non-executive director Ms. Ng Kwok Fun joins the Nomination Committee and existing INED Dr. Ng Chi Yeung, Simon becomes its chairman, signaling a refreshed committee structure intended to maintain regulatory compliance and reinforce independent oversight for shareholders.

The document sets out rules on membership, meeting frequency, quorum, voting, and administrative procedures to strengthen governance and align with Hong Kong Listing Rules and other regulatory standards.

The Nomination Committee must comprise at least three directors, with a majority being independent non-executive directors, and will be chaired by either the board chairman or an independent non-executive director. Meetings are required at least annually or as needed, with detailed provisions on notice, participation by electronic means, written resolutions, and restrictions on members voting on matters concerning their own appointments to reinforce transparency and independence in board nomination processes.

Poly Property Services Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6049) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Poly Property Services Co., Ltd. reported steady growth for 2025, with revenue rising 4.8% to RMB17.13 billion, driven mainly by a 12.6% increase in property management services, while value-added services to non-property owners and community value-added services both declined. The shift underscores the company’s reliance on its core property management segment amid pressure on ancillary service lines.

Gross profit was broadly flat at RMB2.99 billion with a slightly lower gross margin, but profit for the year rose 5.3% to RMB1.57 billion and net margin edged up to 9.2%, reflecting improved profitability. Cash and bank balances grew 8.6% to RMB12.89 billion despite a 20.7% drop in operating cash flow, and the board proposed a 5.2% increase in the annual dividend, maintaining a 50% payout ratio and signaling continued commitment to shareholder returns.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6049) stock is a Buy with a HK$41.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Poly Property Services Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:6049 Stock Forecast page.
